original_title: Cognizant TriZetto breach exposes health data of 3.4 million patients
selection_reasoning: Healthcare breaches represent violations of our most intimate trust - the relationship between healer and patient. This breach affects millions and involves a company that serves as infrastructure for health insurers and providers, making it a critical supply chain failure in one of society's most sensitive domains.

field_note.title: The Sanctuary Compromised
field_note.subtitle: When Healers' Records Fall to Shadow
field_note.excerpt: The sacred trust between healers and those they serve was shattered when shadow infiltrators breached the great ledgers that chronicle 3.4 million souls' most private ailments.
field_note.epigraph: No corruption cuts deeper than when it violates the sacred bond between healer and patient.
body_paragraphs: In the great hierarchy of trust violations, few wounds cut as deeply as the corruption of healing sanctuaries. The recent breach of a vast network that serves as the backbone for countless healers and their guilds has exposed the intimate chronicles of 3.4 million souls-their ailments, their treatments, their most vulnerable moments laid bare to shadow practitioners who had no right to such sacred knowledge.

This was no simple raid on a single healer's chambers, but a systematic compromise of the infrastructure that connects thousands of healing houses and their administrative guilds. The affected network processed the delicate exchanges between healers, their patients, and the complex web of benefactors who fund their care. When such central systems fall, the ripples of violation spread far beyond the immediate victims to touch every relationship in the healing ecosystem.

The breach reveals our dangerous dependency on centralized chroniclers of sensitive memories. In pursuit of efficiency and coordination across vast networks of care, we have concentrated our most private records in systems that become irresistible targets for those who traffic in stolen intimacies. Each patient who trusted their healer also unknowingly placed faith in distant administrators and their protective wards.

What makes this violation particularly grievous is its exploitation of the fundamental covenant of healing-that one's ailments and treatments remain confidential, shared only with those bound by sacred oaths of care. When that trust is broken through negligence or inadequate protection, it damages not just the immediate victims but the entire foundation upon which healing relationships rest. Recovery from such breaches requires rebuilding trust that may take generations to restore.
